name: task-ESE-17
title: Field-level binding selection in generateWgsl()
description:
Currently generateWgsl() binds every field of every component in the kernel's read/write arrays. This creates too many storage buffer bindings — Transform alone has 10 fields. The default WebGPU limit is 8 storage buffers per shader stage. This forced us to create GpuPosition (3 fields) instead of using the shared Transform component.

Fix: the kernel DSL should declare which fields it uses, not just which components. generateWgsl() should only emit bindings for referenced fields. The WGSL body already references specific field names (px, py, pz) — the generator can parse these to determine which fields to bind.

This unblocks using shared Transform in GPU kernels without hitting the storage buffer limit.
acceptanceCriteria:
- GpuKernelDef optionally declares which fields it uses per component
- generateWgsl() only emits bindings for declared fields (or all fields if not specified, for backward compat)
- Buffer upload/readback only transfers declared fields
- Particle kernel can use shared Transform within default 8 storage buffer limit
- Unit tests for field selection, backward compat, and binding count
demoDeliverable: Define a particle kernel that reads/writes Transform (10 fields) + Velocity (3 fields) but only binds px,py,pz,vx,vy,vz (6 fields). Show generateWgsl() produces WGSL with 6 field bindings, not 13. Compile via device.createShaderModule() within default 8 storage buffer limit.
testingScenarios:
- Kernel using Transform + Velocity binds only referenced fields
- Unreferenced fields (rx,ry,rz,rw,sx,sy,sz) excluded from bindings
- Total bindings within default 8 storage buffer limit
- Generated WGSL compiles without errors
- Existing tests still pass (backward compatible)
